sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Levante UD are struggling, with only one win in their last five matches, sitting at the bottom of the league table with 9 points. Real Sociedad, despite inconsistent results, have shown better resilience with two wins in their last five games, positioned at 15th with 16 points. Sociedad's recent ability to score late goals could be a key factor.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last two encounters, Levante UD and Real Sociedad have split the wins, with Levante winning 2-1 at home in May 2022 and Sociedad taking a 1-0 victory in August 2021. Historically, both teams tend to score in these matchups, with 6 of their last 8 meetings seeing both sides on the scoresheet.

sections.subtitle: Team Strengths and Weaknesses
sections.text: Levante UD have struggled defensively, conceding first in 6 of their last 7 games and holding only 2 clean sheets this season. Real Sociedad, while not defensively solid (no clean sheet in last 3 games), have key attacking players like Mikel Oyarzabal in form, contributing 3 goals and 2 assists recently. Levante's low possession (41.5%) and poor pass accuracy (78%) could hinder their ability to control the game.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch
sections.text: For Levante, Ivan Romero's ability to draw fouls (2.8 per game, 3rd in the league) could create set-piece opportunities. Real Sociedad's Mikel Oyarzabal, with a high Sofascore rating and recent goal-scoring form, remains a significant threat, especially given his knack for late contributions.

sections.subtitle: Match Outlook
sections.text: Given Sofascore's prediction (28% home win, 25% draw, 47% away win) and considering current form and league standings, Real Sociedad appear as favorites. Levante's defensive frailties and Sociedad's attacking potential, especially in the second half (highest goal-scoring period), tilt the balance. Expect a competitive match, likely with goals on both sides.

halftimeSections.subtitle: First half summary
halftimeSections.text: The first half at Ciudad de Valencia saw Real Sociedad take a narrow lead over Levante UD with a late goal just before the break. Takefusa Kubo’s header in the 45th minute proved to be the difference, giving the away side a deserved advantage based on their overall control of the game. Levante UD struggled to create meaningful opportunities despite having a decent share of the ball, while Real Sociedad looked more threatening in key moments. Both teams showed discipline with only one yellow card each, but the visitors clearly had the upper hand in terms of chances created.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance and statistics
halftimeSections.text: Real Sociedad dominated possession and showed greater intent in the final third, managing more penalty area entries and creating a couple of big chances, though they missed one. Their expected goals tally reflects a team that was more clinical in attack compared to Levante UD, who failed to create any significant opportunities and registered a very low expected goals figure. Levante UD did show resilience defensively, winning a high percentage of aerial duels, but their lack of cutting edge upfront was evident. Real Sociedad also earned more corner kicks, indicating sustained pressure on the home defense.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players and contributions
halftimeSections.text: Takefusa Kubo was the standout performer for Real Sociedad, not only scoring the crucial goal but also being heavily involved in their attacking play with key passes. Sergio Gómez also played a pivotal role with his ability to create chances from wide areas. For Levante UD, players like Matias Moreno and Unai Vencedor showed defensive solidity with clearances and interceptions, but the team lacked a spark in attack. Carlos Álvarez managed a shot on target, but overall, the home side’s players struggled to make an impact in the final third.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The momentum in the first half swung between both teams at different stages. Levante UD started with some early intent, pushing forward in the opening minutes with a few promising attacking spells. However, Real Sociedad soon took control, building sustained pressure through the middle of the half with multiple phases of dominance in the opposition half. Towards the latter stages, Levante UD tried to regain some initiative, but Real Sociedad finished the half strongly, culminating in their goal just before the whistle. This shift in momentum late on could be a psychological boost for the visitors heading into the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half outlook
halftimeSections.text: Levante UD will need to come out with more aggression in the second half if they are to turn this game around. Trailing by a single goal, they are still in contention but must convert their final third entries into tangible chances. Real Sociedad, on the other hand, will likely look to capitalize on their lead by maintaining possession and exploiting spaces on the counter. Given their superior attacking output in the first half, they seem poised to add to their tally if Levante pushes forward recklessly. Defensive adjustments will be key for the home side to avoid being caught out.
